name: Description & Value Proposition
description: How product descriptions should communicate value and help users understand if the product meets their needs

heuristics.principle: The primary description should explain what the product is and who it is for in plain language
checkpoints: Description answers 'what is this product' clearly
Target use case or audience is evident
Key product purpose is communicated early in description

heuristics.principle: Key benefits should be easy to scan and not buried in long paragraphs
checkpoints: Benefits are presented in scannable format (bullets, icons)
Key features are highlighted or emphasized
Description is not an unbroken wall of text

heuristics.principle: Claims should be supported by evidence such as specifications, reviews or imagery
checkpoints: Quality claims are backed by specifics
Performance claims reference specifications or test data
Social proof supports value claims where available
